title: Asteroid 2022 WJ1 over Lake Erie
narrative: Asteroid 2022 WJ1 was discovered only hours before it entered the atmosphere over Lake Erie on 19 November 2022 — one of a handful of asteroids ever detected before impact. Fireball observations told us when and where to look; MetDetect then picked out WHICH radar echoes are the falling debris. Its top-ranked candidate — the elongated streak shown here, false positives included — is the confirmed debris, scoring 0.97 against a detection threshold of 0.91 set at a 10% false-alarm rate (RUN-30 detector, preliminary calibration). Gate geometry is from the June analysis run; a refresh with the current pipeline is in progress.
